title: chargeback — field classification
description: Chargeback data is a mix of operational identifiers (chargeback_id, chargeback_number), financial records (amount_cents, currency, fee_cents — 7y financial retention), workflow-state metadata (status, submitted_by, decision_at), provider-audit metadata (provider_reference_id, provider_metadata, network reason_code), and evidence bundles (customer PII — IP, device fingerprint hash, service attendance, communication logs). No health / medical / minor-specific data on any row; card PAN / CVV NEVER stored anywhere.

public: Chargeback.status (derived from state machine — safe to expose to admin)
Chargeback.provider (admin-visible — helps admin understand which provider handled the dispute)
confidential: Chargeback.fee_cents (financial-competitive — reveals per-network fee terms + total loss cost)
ChargebackEvidence.invoice_evidence (contains parent invoice detail — line items may include product/service pricing)
ChargebackEvidence.receipt_evidence (contains receipt URL — may embed pricing)
ChargebackEvidence.provider_response (encrypted at rest — provider submission result)
restricted: ChargebackEvidence.service_documentation (contains attendance records + coach signatures — coach/customer PII linkage)
ChargebackEvidence.customer_signature_evidence.device_fingerprint_hash (SHA-256 hash of device fingerprint — not raw fingerprint content)
ChargebackEvidence.shipping_docs (when physical goods — tracking + delivery confirmation includes recipient PII)
ChargebackEvidence.uncategorized_text (may contain any admin-authored freeform notes — potentially PII)
regulated_financial: Chargeback.amount_cents + currency + fee_cents (financial-audit alignment — 7 year retention)
Chargeback.transaction_id + fee_transaction_id (links to double-entry ledger movements — 7y retention)
Chargeback.refund_id (RDR path — links to refund module's ledger)
Chargeback.status (state machine record — every transition audit-logged 7y)
Chargeback.network + reason_code + reason_category (card network audit trail — 7y)
ChargebackEvidence (audit trail — 7y financial + card network audit)

notes: Chargeback.chargeback_number is admin-visible but NOT customer-visible — chargebacks are admin-only surface. The number is used in internal ops + finance reconciliation, never in customer communications.
No PII on the Chargeback row directly. Customer linkage flows through payment_id → payment.customer_id, redacted via the payment module's PII gate.

Chargeback.reason_note field does NOT exist (distinct from refund.reason_note which does). Chargebacks have reason_description (human-readable derived from reason_code) + decision_reason (bank's rationale) + outcome_reason (internal ops summary) — three distinct fields for three distinct sources.
regulated_financial fields align with 7-year retention. Extended to 10y with Enterprise `chargeback_extended_retention`.

PCI-DSS: NO card PAN / CVV / magnetic stripe on any owned row. Chargeback uses payment_id -> payment.provider_reference_id (which is itself a token, never PAN). Evidence submission observer SCANS evidence bodies for PAN patterns + refuses.

provider_metadata + provider_response are CONFIDENTIAL because they may reveal provider-side internals + business relationships. Only finance/admin/owner + support reads them (via chargebacks.view-financials permission).
ChargebackEvidence.customer_signature_evidence.device_fingerprint_hash is stored as SHA-256 hex NEVER as raw fingerprint content — that would leak device-tracking identifiers across sessions.
